title: Search Metadata, Indexing Directives, Canonical Signals, and Language Alternates · Language Clinic: Hreflang Reciprocity
summary: The audit equates crawl permission with indexability, trusts one canonical tag, ignores HTTP robots fields, and reports hreflang without reciprocal or language checks. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the hreflang reciprocity increment in search discovery and indexability audit record. Learners progress through prediction, exp…
objectives: Validate language-region syntax, self and reciprocal links, canonical compatibility, response status, and alternate-set completeness.
Parse page-level robots meta and X-Robots-Tag directives by applicable user agent and separate crawling from indexing and serving.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-hreflang-reciprocity — Validate language-region syntax, self and reciprocal links, canonical compatibility, response status, and alternate-set completeness.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-indexing-canonical-hreflang-3
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-indexing-canonical-hreflang-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def worked_scraper_indexing_canonical_hreflang_3(pages: int = 36, indexing_decisions: int = 36, canonical_conflicts: int = 4, alternate_sets: int = 6, unsupported_index_guarantees: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorizationworked3",
        "url5798",
        "response3dekrow",
        "treeworked35798",
        "frontier5798worked3",
        "report3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or indexing_decisions < 0:
        return False, indexing_decisions, "indexing-count"
    if indexing_decisions != pages:
        return False, indexing_decisions, "indexing-reconciliation"
    if canonical_conflicts < 0 or alternate_sets < 1:
        return False, canonical_conflicts, "search-signal-evidence"
    if unsupported_index_guarantees > 0:
        return False, unsupported_index_guarantees, "index-guarantee"
    assert indexing_decisions == pages and unsupported_index_guarantees == 0
    return True, indexing_decisions, "evidence-based-indexability-audit"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-hreflang-reciprocity-1h4nyr
# Competency: scraper-hreflang-reciprocity.
# Case 9617: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def evidence_scraper_hreflang_reciprocity_1h4nyr(pages: int = 36, indexing_decisions: int = 36, canonical_conflicts: int = 4, alternate_sets: int = 6, unsupported_index_guarantees: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1h4nyr = "1h4nyr-ryn4h1-9617"
    assert evidence_variant_1h4nyr
    evidence_axes_1h4nyr = (
        "authorization1h4nyr",
        "url9617",
        "responseryn4h1",
        "tree1h4nyr9617",
        "frontier96171h4nyr",
        "reportryn4h19617",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1h4nyr)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or indexing_decisions < 0:
        return False, indexing_decisions, "indexing-count"
    if indexing_decisions != pages:
        return False, indexing_decisions, "indexing-reconciliation"
    if canonical_conflicts < 0 or alternate_sets < 1:
        return False, canonical_conflicts, "search-signal-evidence"
    if unsupported_index_guarantees > 0:
        return False, unsupported_index_guarantees, "index-guarantee"
    assert indexing_decisions == pages and unsupported_index_guarantees == 0
    return True, indexing_decisions, "evidence-based-indexability-audit"

options.text: The Hreflang Reciprocity record says search discovery and indexability audit record looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect hreflang reciprocity evidence boundary for Hreflang Reciprocity.

For inspect hreflang reciprocity evidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof.
